name: Two Bays Green Corridor Trail
name_original: Corredor Verde de Dos Bahías – circular desde Puerto Real
description: Cycle the easy 16.1-mile Green Corridor of Two Bays circular from Puerto Real, exploring salt marshes, pine forests, and open countryside.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Puerto Real offer diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Bay of Cádiz to inland trails with varied terrain. The region features a mix of well-paved surfaces and unpaved segments, catering to different cycling preferences. Cyclists can explore the unique ecosystems of the Bahía de Cádiz National Park and scenic lagoon areas. Some routes extend inland, providing moderate elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Puerto Real The most popular touring cycling route is Two Bays Green Corridor Trail, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the coastline and the Bay of Cádiz. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castillo de San Marcos – Bridge Over the Río San Pedro loop from Puerto Real, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.6 km) path. This route provides maritime scenery along the Bay of Cádiz. Local touring cyclists also love the El Carrascón Trail – Access to the cycle track loop from Barrio de Jarana, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Puerto Real is defined by coastal paths, the Bay of Cádiz, and varied inland terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging routes with elevation gains. The routes in Puerto Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 55 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Puerto Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Puerto Real?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in and around Puerto Real, offering a wide variety of options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 55 reviews.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Puerto Real?
A: Yes, Puerto Real offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980576136/two-bays-green-corridor-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Two Bays Green Corridor Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 26.0 km route that takes less than two hours to complete, providing scenic views of the coastline and the Bay of Cádiz.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Puerto Real?
A: Touring cycling routes around Puerto Real feature diverse landscapes. You'll find a mix of well-paved surfaces suitable for road cycling, especially along the coast and through towns. Some routes also include unpaved segments for those seeking a more rugged experience or gravel biking. Routes extending inland, such as those towards Arcos de la Frontera, may include more significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Puerto Real are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31836639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Marcos – Bridge Over the Río San Pedro loop from Puerto Real&lt;/a&gt;, which offers maritime scenery along the Bay of Cádiz.

Q: What are some notable natural features or parks I can cycle through?
A: The Bahía de Cádiz National Park is a significant natural draw, offering unique ecosystems to explore by bike. You can also find scenic lagoon areas, such as those near the Medina Lagoon, providing diverse aquatic landscapes beyond the immediate coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33942795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Carrascón Trail – Access to the cycle track loop from Barrio de Jarana&lt;/a&gt; is another route that leads through varied terrain, often showcasing the region's natural beauty.

Q: Can I combine cycling with exploring charming towns and cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes connect Puerto Real with other picturesque towns in the region, such as El Puerto de Santa María, Arcos de la Frontera, and Medina Sidonia. These routes offer opportunities to experience local culture, admire historic architecture, and visit landmarks like the Prioral de San Sebastián in Puerto Real or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1052801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Castle&lt;/a&gt; in Cádiz.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the touring cycling routes in the wider Cádiz area?
A: Beyond Puerto Real, you can explore several fascinating attractions. Consider visiting the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4901953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cádiz Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; or the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4379436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral Square, Cádiz&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4902517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda de Apodaca Promenade&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning views, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4379413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genovés Park&lt;/a&gt; provides a lovely green space.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Bay of Cádiz?
A: Many routes in Puerto Real are designed to showcase the stunning Bay of Cádiz. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31549596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Río San Pedro loop from Universidad&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that provides excellent maritime scenery and views of the bay.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Puerto Real?
A: Puerto Real, located in Andalusia, Spain, generally offers pleasant weather for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide comfortable temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even winter can be suitable, with milder conditions compared to many other European regions, making it a good option for year-round cycling.

Q: Are there any longer touring routes that connect Puerto Real with other towns?
A: Yes, cyclists can embark on longer tours that link Puerto Real with other charming towns. Routes extend to places like Arcos de la Frontera, Medina Sidonia, San José del Valle, and El Puerto de Santa María, offering a chance to explore more of the region's diverse landscapes and culture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas Osborne – Río San Pedro Cycle Path loop from Puerto Real&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33.5 km route that can be part of a longer exploration.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Puerto Real unique?
A: Touring cycling in Puerto Real is unique due to its blend of picturesque coastal paths along the Bay of Cádiz, access to the distinct ecosystems of the Bahía de Cádiz National Park, and varied inland terrain. The ability to connect with charming Andalusian towns and experience both maritime and rural landscapes makes it a highly appealing destination for cyclists of all levels.
tips: Nice track for cycling - OK for hybrid bikes

faqs.question: How challenging is the Green Corridor of Two Bays cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route from Puerto Real is considered easy, making it suitable for cyclists of all fitness levels. It features minimal elevation gain and a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, perfect for a relaxed touring bicycle ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. You'll find mostly paved surfaces, including paths, cycleways, roads, and streets, totaling about 23.4 km. There are also unpaved sections (12.7 km), compacted gravel (2.27 km), and asphalt (5.12 km), providing a diverse cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: Starting in Puerto Real, you'll experience the beautiful salt marshes and wetlands of the Parque Natural de la Bahía de Cádiz. The route then transitions through peaceful pine forests, particularly those with 'pinos piñoneros', before opening up to the expansive, gently rolling landscapes of the 'campiña'. Don't miss the Green Corridor of Two Bays itself and the lovely Parque de las Cañadas near the start.

faqs.question: Is the Green Corridor of Two Bays - circular from Puerto Real dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, as parts of the route pass through the Parque Natural de la Bahía de Cádiz, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route or access the Parque Natural de la Bahía de Cádiz. It's freely accessible for recreational use.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. The weather is usually mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This allows for comfortable cycling and enjoying the natural scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Green Corridor of Two Bays circular route from Puerto Real?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Puerto Real, especially near the starting point of the route, which is close to the Parque de las Cañadas. Look for public parking areas within the town that offer convenient access to the trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the larger 'Corredor Verde Dos Bahías' and intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Corredor Verde Dos Bahías, Corredor Verde de las Dos Bahías, Camino de Santiago del Estrecho, and Transandalus, among others. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself passes through natural areas, Puerto Real, your starting and ending point, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and restrooms. It's advisable to stock up on water and snacks before you set off, especially for the sections away from the town.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see in the Parque Natural de la Bahía de Cádiz?
faqs.answer: As the route traverses the Parque Natural de la Bahía de Cádiz, you might spot a variety of birdlife, especially in the salt marshes and wetlands. Keep an eye out for flamingos, avocets, and other migratory birds. The pine forests also host various smaller birds and insects.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Given its easy difficulty, minimal elevation, and mostly well-maintained surfaces, this circular route is very suitable for families with children. It's a great way to enjoy a day out cycling together in nature.
